test_cases.description: Verifies that the web/ directory exists at the repo root and contains its own package.json and tsconfig.json, with no shared config files with the Go backend.
preconditions: Repository has been initialized with the web UI scaffold
The Go backend exists at the repo root
test_cases.input: Repository file system after scaffold setup
test_cases.expected: Directory `web/` exists at repo root; `web/package.json` and `web/tsconfig.json` are present; no shared tsconfig or package.json exists between `web/` and the Go backend root
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert path_exists('web/')
assert path_exists('web/package.json')
assert path_exists('web/tsconfig.json')
assert NOT path_exists('tsconfig.json') OR tsconfig_at_root_is_not_shared_with_web()
assert read_json('web/package.json')['name'] is not null

test_cases.description: Verifies that package.json declares Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Router, and TanStack Query as dependencies or devDependencies.
test_cases.expected: package.json lists vite, react, react-dom, typescript, tailwindcss, react-router-dom (or react-router v6+), and @tanstack/react-query in dependencies or devDependencies
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = read_json('web/package.json')
all_deps = merge(pkg.get('dependencies', {}), pkg.get('devDependencies', {}))
assert 'vite' in all_deps
assert 'react' in all_deps
assert 'react-dom' in all_deps
assert 'typescript' in all_deps
assert 'tailwindcss' in all_deps
assert any(k in all_deps for k in ['react-router-dom', 'react-router'])
assert '@tanstack/react-query' in all_deps

test_cases.description: Verifies that shadcn/ui components are copied into the project tree and are NOT listed as an npm dependency in package.json.
preconditions: `web/` directory has been scaffolded with `npx shadcn-ui@latest init`
test_cases.expected: shadcn/ui is not present in dependencies or devDependencies; shadcn/ui component source files (e.g., `web/src/components/ui/`) exist in the project tree
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = read_json('web/package.json')
all_deps = merge(pkg.get('dependencies', {}), pkg.get('devDependencies', {}))
assert 'shadcn-ui' NOT in all_deps
assert '@shadcn/ui' NOT in all_deps
assert directory_exists('web/src/components/ui') OR any(glob('web/src/components/ui/*.tsx'))

test_cases.description: Verifies that the project enforces or documents a minimum Node.js version of 18 or later.
test_cases.expected: Either `engines.node` in package.json specifies `>=18` or `docs/web-ui.md` explicitly states the Node 18+ requirement
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = read_json('web/package.json')
engines_ok = pkg.get('engines', {}).get('node', '').contains('18') OR pkg.get('engines', {}).get('node', '').contains('>=')
docs_content = read_file('docs/web-ui.md')
docs_ok = 'Node 18' in docs_content OR 'Node.js 18' in docs_content OR '>=18' in docs_content
assert engines_ok OR docs_ok

test_cases.description: Verifies that QueryClientProvider from TanStack Query wraps the root React application component tree.
test_cases.input: Source file of the React application entry point (e.g., `web/src/main.tsx`)
test_cases.expected: The entry point imports QueryClientProvider and QueryClient from @tanstack/react-query and wraps the App (or root component) with <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
src = read_file('web/src/main.tsx')
assert 'QueryClientProvider' in src
assert 'QueryClient' in src
assert '@tanstack/react-query' in src
assert src contains pattern '<QueryClientProvider' before or wrapping '<App' or root router component

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Vite dev proxy configuration forwards /api/* requests to http://localhost:8080 preserving the path.
test_cases.expected: vite.config.ts contains a proxy entry for `/api` (or `/api/`) targeting `http://localhost:8080` with changeOrigin enabled and no path rewriting that would drop the `/api` prefix
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
config = read_file('web/vite.config.ts')
assert 'localhost:8080' in config
assert '/api' in config
proxy_section = extract_proxy_config(config)
api_rule = proxy_section['/api']
assert api_rule.target == 'http://localhost:8080'
assert api_rule does NOT strip '/api' prefix (no rewrite to remove '/api')

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Vite dev proxy configuration forwards /healthz requests to http://localhost:8080/healthz.
test_cases.expected: vite.config.ts contains a proxy entry for `/healthz` targeting `http://localhost:8080`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
config = read_file('web/vite.config.ts')
proxy_section = extract_proxy_config(config)
healthz_rule = proxy_section['/healthz']
assert healthz_rule.target == 'http://localhost:8080'

test_cases.description: Verifies that the Vite dev proxy configuration forwards /readyz requests to http://localhost:8080/readyz.
test_cases.expected: vite.config.ts contains a proxy entry for `/readyz` targeting `http://localhost:8080`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
config = read_file('web/vite.config.ts')
proxy_section = extract_proxy_config(config)
readyz_rule = proxy_section['/readyz']
assert readyz_rule.target == 'http://localhost:8080'

test_cases.description: Verifies that vite.config.ts defines all three proxy rules (/api/*, /healthz, /readyz) targeting port 8080.
test_cases.expected: Proxy section contains entries for `/api` (or `/api/*`), `/healthz`, and `/readyz`, all pointing to `http://localhost:8080`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
config = read_file('web/vite.config.ts')
proxy_section = extract_proxy_config(config)
assert '/api' in proxy_section OR any key starting with '/api' in proxy_section
assert '/healthz' in proxy_section
assert '/readyz' in proxy_section
for each rule in [proxy_section['/api'], proxy_section['/healthz'], proxy_section['/readyz']]:
  assert rule.target == 'http://localhost:8080'

test_cases.description: Verifies that the make web-dev target installs dependencies when node_modules/ is absent and then starts the Vite dev server.
preconditions: Makefile exists at repo root with a `web-dev` target
test_cases.input: Running `make web-dev` from the repo root
test_cases.expected: `npm install` is executed in `web/` first, then `npm run dev` is executed in `web/`; Vite dev server starts and prints a local URL to stdout
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
makefile_content = read_file('Makefile')
web_dev_target = extract_target(makefile_content, 'web-dev')
assert 'npm install' in web_dev_target OR 'node_modules' check present triggering install
assert 'npm run dev' in web_dev_target
assert install_step appears before dev_step in target

test_cases.description: Verifies that the make web-build target installs dependencies when node_modules/ is absent and then runs the production build outputting to web/dist/.
preconditions: Makefile exists at repo root with a `web-build` target
test_cases.input: Running `make web-build` from the repo root
test_cases.expected: `npm install` runs first in `web/`, then `npm run build` runs in `web/`; static assets are emitted to `web/dist/` on success; make exits with code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
makefile_content = read_file('Makefile')
web_build_target = extract_target(makefile_content, 'web-build')
assert 'npm install' in web_build_target OR 'node_modules' check present triggering install
assert 'npm run build' in web_build_target
assert install_step appears before build_step in target

test_cases.description: Verifies that npm run dev starts the Vite dev server with hot module replacement enabled.
test_cases.expected: The `dev` script invokes `vite` (e.g., `"dev": "vite"`); Vite starts a dev server with HMR when executed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = read_json('web/package.json')
assert 'dev' in pkg['scripts']
dev_cmd = pkg['scripts']['dev']
assert 'vite' in dev_cmd
assert dev_cmd does not include '--no-hmr' or similar HMR-disabling flags

test_cases.description: Verifies that npm run build runs the Vite production build and emits compiled assets to web/dist/.
test_cases.expected: The `build` script invokes `vite build`; on success, compiled assets appear in `web/dist/`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = read_json('web/package.json')
assert 'build' in pkg['scripts']
build_cmd = pkg['scripts']['build']
assert 'vite build' in build_cmd OR ('tsc' in build_cmd AND 'vite build' in build_cmd)
assert outDir is 'dist' (from vite.config.ts or default)

test_cases.description: Verifies that npm run lint runs ESLint and TypeScript type checking via tsc --noEmit.
test_cases.expected: The `lint` script contains both an ESLint invocation and a TypeScript type check (`tsc --noEmit`); running it on valid code exits with code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = read_json('web/package.json')
assert 'lint' in pkg['scripts']
lint_cmd = pkg['scripts']['lint']
assert 'eslint' in lint_cmd
assert 'tsc' in lint_cmd AND '--noEmit' in lint_cmd

test_cases.description: Verifies that npm run preview starts a local static server serving the web/dist/ output.
test_cases.expected: The `preview` script invokes `vite preview`; it serves the built assets from `web/dist/`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
pkg = read_json('web/package.json')
assert 'preview' in pkg['scripts']
preview_cmd = pkg['scripts']['preview']
assert 'vite preview' in preview_cmd

test_cases.description: Verifies that navigating to / renders a page displaying 'af-hub' text and a placeholder message with minimal layout.
preconditions: `web/` project is built or dev server is running
React Router is configured with a route at `/`
test_cases.input: Browser or test renderer navigates to `/`
test_cases.expected: The rendered HTML contains the text 'af-hub' and at least one placeholder message string; no navigation bar, sidebar, or complex layout components are present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
rendered = render_component(App, route='/')
assert text_content_contains(rendered, 'af-hub')
assert text_content_contains(rendered, placeholder_message)  // any non-empty placeholder string
assert NOT rendered_contains_nav_or_sidebar(rendered)

test_cases.description: Verifies that React Router is configured with exactly one route mapping '/' to the Hello World page component.
preconditions: Router configuration file or App component source exists in `web/src/`
test_cases.expected: The router definition contains a route with path '/' pointing to the Hello World (or equivalent index) component; no other routes are defined in this scaffold
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
router_source = find_router_config('web/src/')
assert route_exists(router_source, path='/')
route = get_route(router_source, path='/')
assert route.component is not null
assert route.component renders 'af-hub' text

test_cases.description: Verifies that docs/web-ui.md covers all required topics: Node 18+ prerequisites, npm install, dev server, production build, project structure, and shadcn/ui conventions.
test_cases.expected: The file contains sections or mentions of: Node.js 18+ requirement, `npm install`, `npm run dev`, `npm run build`, the `web/` directory structure, and shadcn/ui component conventions (copy-into-tree model)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
doc = read_file('docs/web-ui.md')
assert 'Node' in doc AND ('18' in doc OR '>=18' in doc)
assert 'npm install' in doc
assert 'npm run dev' in doc
assert 'npm run build' in doc
assert 'web/' in doc
assert 'shadcn' in doc
assert any phrase like 'copied' OR 'copy' OR 'npx shadcn' in doc

test_cases.description: Verifies that README.md links to docs/web-ui.md and mentions the make web-dev and make web-build targets.
test_cases.expected: README.md contains a link or reference to `docs/web-ui.md`, and mentions `make web-dev` and `make web-build`
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
readme = read_file('README.md')
assert 'docs/web-ui.md' in readme OR 'web-ui' in readme
assert 'make web-dev' in readme OR 'web-dev' in readme
assert 'make web-build' in readme OR 'web-build' in readme

property_tests.description: For any request path starting with /api/, or exactly /healthz or /readyz, vite.config.ts must have a matching proxy rule targeting http://localhost:8080.
property_tests.invariant_check:
config = read_file('web/vite.config.ts')
proxy = extract_proxy_config(config)
for path in ['/api/anything', '/api/users', '/api/v1/resource', '/healthz', '/readyz']:
  matching_rule = find_matching_proxy_rule(proxy, path)
  assert matching_rule is not null, f'No proxy rule for {path}'
  assert matching_rule.target == 'http://localhost:8080'

property_tests.description: For any invocation of make web-dev or make web-build where node_modules/ is absent, npm install always runs before any Vite command, ensuring no unbounded retry or skipped install.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Two scenarios: (1) node_modules/ absent, (2) node_modules/ present — for both make web-dev and make web-build

property_tests.description: For any npm script that exits with a non-zero code, the exit code is propagated to the calling process without being masked or swallowed.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Simulate non-zero exits for npm run dev, npm run build, and npm run lint by injecting exit-code-returning stubs

property_tests.description: For any browser navigation to '/', the Hello World page always renders 'af-hub' text and a placeholder message with no uncaught JavaScript exceptions.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Render the App component with route '/' using a test renderer; repeat across build (production) and dev modes
property_tests.invariant_check:
for mode in ['dev', 'production']:
  rendered = render_app_at_route('/', mode=mode)
  assert text_content_contains(rendered, 'af-hub')
  assert has_placeholder_message(rendered)
  assert no_uncaught_exceptions()
  assert rendered.html() != ''

property_tests.description: For any React component rendered within the application, it is always a descendant of QueryClientProvider, ensuring TanStack Query hooks are universally available.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Inspect the React component tree rendered from the application entry point; check ancestry of any arbitrarily nested component
property_tests.invariant_check:
entry = read_file('web/src/main.tsx')
// Static analysis: QueryClientProvider appears as an ancestor before App or Router
assert 'QueryClientProvider' in entry
assert 'QueryClient' in entry
// Verify wrapping order in source
qcp_pos = index_of(entry, 'QueryClientProvider')
app_pos = index_of(entry, '<App') OR index_of(entry, '<RouterProvider') OR index_of(entry, '<BrowserRouter')
assert qcp_pos < app_pos  // QueryClientProvider opens before App
// Runtime check
tree = render_app()
assert find_ancestor(tree, component_type='QueryClientProvider') is not null

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when Node.js older than version 18 is used, npm or Vite emits a clear incompatibility error, and that docs/web-ui.md documents the Node 18+ prerequisite.
preconditions: A Node.js version older than 18 (e.g., Node 16) is active in the environment
edge_case_tests.input: Run `npm install` or `npm run dev` in `web/` with Node.js < 18
edge_case_tests.expected: npm or Vite exits with a non-zero code and prints a message indicating the Node.js version is incompatible; `docs/web-ui.md` contains explicit Node 18+ prerequisite documentation

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when the Go backend is not running, the Vite dev server returns a 502/connection-refused error to the browser without crashing.
preconditions: Vite dev server is running with proxy configured
Go backend at http://localhost:8080 is NOT running
edge_case_tests.expected: The browser receives an HTTP 502 or connection-refused error response; the Vite dev server process continues running and responds to subsequent requests
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Vite proxy error behavior check
config = read_file('web/vite.config.ts')
proxy_config = extract_proxy_config(config)
api_rule = proxy_config['/api']
assert api_rule.target == 'http://localhost:8080'
// Behavioral check
vite_server = start_vite_dev_server(backend_running=False)
response = http_get(vite_server.url + '/api/test')
assert response.status_code in [502, 503, 504] OR response.is_connection_error
assert vite_server.is_running()
assert vite_server.responds_to_non_proxy_requests()

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that make web-build propagates a non-zero exit code when npm run build fails due to TypeScript or bundling errors.
preconditions: A TypeScript error exists in the source that will cause `npm run build` to fail
edge_case_tests.input: Running `make web-build` when `npm run build` exits non-zero
edge_case_tests.expected: make exits with the same non-zero exit code as npm; error output from npm/Vite is visible on stderr; `web/dist/` is either absent or incomplete
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
makefile = read_file('Makefile')
web_build = extract_target(makefile, 'web-build')
// Check the target does not swallow exit codes
assert NOT uses_dash_prefix_to_ignore_errors(web_build)
assert NOT 'exit 0' forced after npm command
// Behavioral simulation
result = run_make('web-build', with_build_error=True)
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert result.stderr contains error output

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when npm install fails, make web-dev and make web-build propagate the non-zero exit code and do not proceed to the dev or build step.
preconditions: npm install is simulated to fail (e.g., mock npm registry unavailable)
edge_case_tests.input: Running `make web-dev` or `make web-build` when `npm install` exits non-zero
edge_case_tests.expected: make exits with a non-zero code; npm error output is visible on stderr; neither `npm run dev` nor `npm run build` is executed after the failed install
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
makefile = read_file('Makefile')
for target in ['web-dev', 'web-build']:
  target_body = extract_target(makefile, target)
  assert install_step_comes_before_run_step(target_body)
  assert NOT ignores_install_exit_code(target_body)
// Behavioral simulation
result = run_make('web-dev', npm_install_fails=True)
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert 'npm run dev' NOT in result.commands_executed
result2 = run_make('web-build', npm_install_fails=True)
assert result2.exit_code != 0
assert 'npm run build' NOT in result2.commands_executed

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that npm run lint prints all errors with file names and line numbers and exits with a non-zero code when TypeScript or ESLint errors exist.
preconditions: A TypeScript source file in `web/src/` contains a deliberate type error or ESLint violation
edge_case_tests.input: Running `npm run lint` in `web/` with source errors present
edge_case_tests.expected: Command exits with a non-zero exit code; output includes file names and line numbers for each error; no errors are silently swallowed
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Introduce a deliberate error
write_file('web/src/__test_error__.tsx', 'const x: number = "not a number";')
result = run_command('npm run lint', cwd='web/')
delete_file('web/src/__test_error__.tsx')
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert '__test_error__' in result.output
assert line_number_present_in_output(result.output)
assert 'number' in result.output OR 'string' in result.output

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that navigating to an unknown route (e.g., /unknown) does not crash the React application and renders a non-blank page.
preconditions: React app is running with React Router configured
edge_case_tests.expected: The application renders a non-blank page (either a fallback/404 indicator or graceful redirect); no unhandled JavaScript exceptions are thrown in the browser console
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
rendered = render_component(App, route='/unknown')
assert rendered is not null
assert rendered.html() != ''
assert no_uncaught_exceptions_thrown()
assert NOT react_error_boundary_triggered(rendered)

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test verifying that a developer can run make web-dev from the repo root, have dependencies auto-installed, and see the Hello World page rendered in a browser.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer runs `make web-dev` from the repository root with `web/node_modules/` absent
real_components: web/src/ React app and Hello World route component
mockable: Go backend at http://localhost:8080 (not required for this smoke test; proxy rules exist but backend need not respond)
expected_effects: Vite dev server starts and prints a local URL (e.g., http://localhost:5173) to stdout
HTTP GET to the printed URL returns HTTP 200
No JavaScript console errors on initial page load

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test verifying that make web-build produces a successful production build with static assets in web/dist/.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer runs `make web-build` from the repository root with `web/node_modules/` absent
mockable: Go backend (not needed for build step)
expected_effects: Vite compiles TypeScript and bundles assets with no errors
`web/dist/` directory is created and contains `index.html` and bundled JS/CSS assets

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test verifying that the Vite dev proxy correctly forwards /api/* requests to the Go backend and returns the response to the browser.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer issues a browser request to http://localhost:5173/api/some-endpoint while both the Vite dev server and Go backend are running
real_components: Go backend server (spec 01) running at http://localhost:8080
mockable: Specific API endpoint handler on the Go backend (can use /healthz as a simple proxy verification target)
expected_effects: Vite dev server matches the /api/* proxy rule from vite.config.ts
Go backend receives the request and returns an HTTP response
Browser receives the response with no CORS errors
Response status code and body match what the Go backend returned
